run.notes: pedantic_nitpicker@codex timed out at 900s (0 concerns; PEDA covered by gemini). FLOW@gemini 9 concerns recovered manually from raw responses (parser missed bolded list numbers) and patched into checkpoint before resume. Claude one-pass synthesis below is the authoritative verdict set; pipeline verdicts were advisory.

synthesis.evaluator: claude (one Opus-class pass, all 669 concerns read, all verdict classes included)

correctness_bugs.title: S7/INV-13 supersession contradiction
correctness_bugs.detail: State table restricts S7 supersession to pre-judgment states; INV-13 says judged-pass rows exit ONLY via S7. Direct contradiction. Fix: S7 allowed from any state including judged-pass, always human-approved; judged-fail correction = reset (S6) vs redesign (S7) decision rule added.

correctness_bugs.title: Sample row shows result:null with populated judgment
correctness_bugs.detail: Violates INV-1. Fix example; add bidirectional rule: result == null iff judgment == null.

correctness_bugs.title: digest_batches schema missing lifecycle fields
correctness_bugs.detail: Prose requires open/closed/cancelled status, row-hash snapshots, cancellation audit, stale-reply checks; sample JSON has only digest_id/parts/sent_at/row_ids. Add: status, opened_at/closed_at/cancelled_at, cancel_reason, row_hash_snapshot{}, conops_hash_snapshot, part file paths+hashes, per-part send results+message ids.

correctness_bugs.title: State machine missing cancel-batch and ConOps-edit transitions
correctness_bugs.detail: Add S8: digested -> evidence-attached on batch cancel (audit fields); add hash-invalidation transition for ConOps edits per refresh rules; distinguish 'drafted' vs 'needs-reexecution' after S6 reset.

correctness_bugs.title: Idempotent re-entry skip-to-MCP bypasses INV-5
correctness_bugs.detail: Re-entry with existing clean system_validation.json must STILL re-run self-check immediately before the MCP call (INV-5 wins). Also fail-rows-present re-entry must route to remediation, not NOTHING_TO_DIGEST->emission loop.

correctness_bugs.title: Ledger mutator list inconsistent; supersession and drafting have no owning command
correctness_bugs.detail: 6.2 names 2 mutators, 7 names 3, S7 supersession and Phase 7 row drafting have none (drafting is raw hand-editing that bypasses lock discipline). Add supersede-row and normalize-rows (hash-stamping/draft) subcommands; mutator list becomes parse-reply, assemble-digest, cancel-batch, reset-failed, supersede-row, normalize-rows.

correctness_bugs.title: row_hash has no producer (LLM cannot compute sha256)
correctness_bugs.detail: check-rows demands canonical hashes but no command computes them; conductor would hallucinate hex. normalize-rows (or check-rows --fix) computes/stamps row_hash.

correctness_bugs.title: Unclosed-batch deadlock on partial-fail remediation re-entry
correctness_bugs.detail: parse-reply closes batch when all snapshot rows judged; partially-judged batch + remediation requires explicit cancel-batch before re-assembly — add to Phase 8 fail path. Define multi-reply continuation flow for open batches.

correctness_bugs.title: Reply grammar ambiguities
correctness_bugs.detail: Reject fail rest/na rest explicitly; explicit verdicts always apply before pass all/rest regardless of textual order; pass-with-text stored as justification; ALL duplicate row verdicts in one reply rejected (drop 'unless identical'); continuation lines must not begin with a verdict token (else parse error, not justification); case-insensitive verdict keywords, exact-case row ids; whitespace normalization defined.

correctness_bugs.title: Coverage matching: exact-token rule needed locally
correctness_bugs.detail: Gate substring-matches conops_ref (US-1 satisfied by US-11; packed refs). Local rule: conops_ref must regex-match ^US-\d+$ exactly one id; local coverage uses exact-token equality — stated as strictly-stricter than gate.

correctness_bugs.title: ConOps regex leak: stray US-n in narrative creates phantom gate obligations
correctness_bugs.detail: derive-conops lints: every \bUS-\d+\b token in output must correspond to a manifest story heading; reject otherwise. self-check already mirrors whole-file regex (kept).

correctness_bugs.title: Ledger result enum and na conversion undefined
correctness_bugs.detail: Define ledger enum pass|fail|not-applicable; parse-reply maps reply token na -> not-applicable; projection passes through.

race_conditions.title: TOCTOU between self-check and MCP call
race_conditions.detail: self-check outputs content sha256; close step re-verifies hash immediately before mark_system_validation_complete; reject symlinked artifact paths.

race_conditions.title: Batch 'open' before delivery confirmed; pass all can approve unseen rows
race_conditions.detail: Biggest flow hole (convergent across 5 adversaries). Fix: batch states assembled -> sent -> (closed|cancelled); per-part send results + message ids recorded via new record-send subcommand (or args to assemble flow); parse-reply rejects bulk verdicts (pass all/rest) unless batch status == sent (all parts).

race_conditions.title: Batch does not snapshot conops_hash
race_conditions.detail: Snapshot conops_hash at batch open; reply rejected if ConOps changed since open.

race_conditions.title: Duplicate/edited inbound delivery not idempotent
race_conditions.detail: parse-reply rejects already-processed reply_ref (idempotency by reply_ref recorded in batch); edited Telegram messages ignored (first capture wins); one message per parse-reply invocation.

race_conditions.title: Phase 7->8 gap drift unguarded (OQ-3)
race_conditions.detail: RESOLVE OQ-3: Phase 8 close entry always re-derives ConOps and compares hash; mismatch -> refresh protocol before any evidence work. Phase 7 ledger baseline hash recorded at commit (drafted_baseline_hash) and compared at Phase 8 entry.

failure_modes.title: Remediation card creation/resolution has no producer and is unverifiable pure-local
failure_modes.detail: Reframe honestly: CONDUCTOR creates cards via existing fizzy MCP (add_card/pipeline tools) with required payload {row_id, conops_ref, scenario, oracle, evidence_ref, fail justification, digest_id}; CONDUCTOR verifies resolution via MCP before invoking reset-failed; module records remediation_ref as assertion (documented as conductor-verified, not module-verified). TC-3.8 reworded accordingly.

failure_modes.title: Evidence hash headers in free markdown are brittle; no evidence producer command
failure_modes.detail: Canonical YAML front-matter block (exact keys: row_hash, conops_hash, evidence_type, produced_at, commit) — duplicates rejected; new record-evidence subcommand scaffolds the file with stamped hashes so LLM never formats hashes by hand; distinct error codes for missing vs malformed vs mismatched headers.

failure_modes.title: ConOps hash blast radius: one story edit orphans ALL evidence
failure_modes.detail: Add per-story section hash (sha256 of the US-n heading+body): evidence binds to story_hash + row_hash; global conops_hash retained for the gate artifact only. Story edit invalidates only affected rows' evidence.

failure_modes.title: Remediation does not force fresh evidence; stale evidence revalidates
failure_modes.detail: S6 reset moves evidence to evidence.md.invalidated-<ts> (cannot be re-accepted); evidence front-matter embeds implementation commit hash; assemble-digest rejects evidence whose produced_at predates the reset.

failure_modes.title: No status command; no local error playbook; stdout envelope undefined
failure_modes.detail: Add read-only status subcommand (active batch, sent parts, unjudged rows, blockers, next action); 10 gains a local-codes table (LEDGER_BUSY, LEDGER_CORRUPT, NOTHING_TO_DIGEST, SENDER_NOT_ALLOWLISTED, EVIDENCE_*, STALE_DIGEST, REPROMPT_REQUIRED, REFRESH_DISALLOWED); exact stdout JSON envelope defined for success/issues/reprompt (row_id nullable for global issues).

failure_modes.title: Telegram send/listen failure semantics absent
failure_modes.detail: Per-part send: bounded retry (3x backoff) then fail with parts-sent state recorded; mixed-delivery rule (cancel + terminal fallback covers WHOLE batch, never split channels); inbound: no listener liveness guarantee — document stale-open-batch age warning in status output.

failure_modes.title: Recovery: restore-from-git loses uncommitted judgments
failure_modes.detail: Commit cadence: commit ledger after every batch close and before MCP call; LEDGER_CORRUPT response = quarantine corrupt bytes aside (ledger.corrupt-<ts>) THEN restore; replay from quarantine + Telegram transcript if needed.

failure_modes.title: MCP close wiring not concrete
failure_modes.detail: Close preflight (before Jason is paged): board_id from projects.yaml, card_id+session_id from session detail file, get_card_metadata altitude/session match, conops/artifact paths exist+readable. Post-call: read metadata back to confirm system_validation_complete. Transient MCP error: bounded retry then metadata check (handles lost-response). get_card_metadata availability verified during implementation (it exists in fizzy toolset).

failure_modes.title: Oversized scenario/oracle breaks 3500-char part budget
failure_modes.detail: check-rows lints scenario+oracle+header <= 3000 chars per row (room for furniture); over-budget row is a draft error, never a runtime delivery failure.

failure_modes.title: fail justification not required (only na)
failure_modes.detail: fail requires non-empty justification (remediation cards depend on it); bare fail -> re-prompt.

failure_modes.title: Sole-row N/A discovered only at close
failure_modes.detail: parse-reply warns immediately when an na verdict leaves a story with zero pass rows (early surface; close rule unchanged).

failure_modes.title: Stale reply to cancelled batch: human-facing consequence undefined
failure_modes.detail: On cancel of an already-sent batch: conductor sends cancellation notice through same channel; stale replies get a rejection notice referencing the new digest.

security.title: Sender identity is a conductor-typed CLI flag — the trust boundary is a sieve
security.detail: Jason ruling 2026-06-11: right-sized. Threat is NOT account takeover — it is the conductor LLM typing --sender-id itself, making the check decorative against agent confusion (wrong message applied as judgment). Fix kept: parse-reply reads the raw wake-listener update file (--update-file) and extracts sender/message id itself. The 'fail-closed until verified' framing is DROPPED — verifying the listener payload schema is an ordinary first implementation task (~10 min reading listener source), not a blocking gate.

security.title: Chat id conflated with sender id; allowlist schema/fail-closed undefined
security.detail: Registry config defines BOTH chat_id (channel scope) and allowed_sender_ids (human allowlist); exact file path + field names specified; missing/malformed config -> Telegram replies rejected (fail-closed) with structured code.

security.title: Terminal fallback is assertion, not authentication
security.detail: Keep single-user assumption (documented) but require reply_ref = AskUserQuestion transcript reference and add negative test for terminal-source abuse; spec stops implying parity with Telegram allowlist.

security.title: Digest content hygiene: secrets, escaping, reply-quoting injection
security.detail: assemble-digest runs deny-pattern secret lint (key/token/bearer/env shapes) over digest text — match blocks assembly; row prose escaped so it cannot spoof digest furniture or reply instructions; re-prompt quotes offending text truncated to 200 chars with untrusted-content framing; evidence paths emitted repo-relative.

security.title: What Jason judged is not hash-bound: digest parts and evidence content
security.detail: Batch records sha256 of each part file; evidence content hash recorded in ledger at digest assembly; closed batch records applied reply text hash. Cheap, makes 'what did Jason see' answerable.

security.title: pass all is a low-friction bulk approval
security.detail: Jason ruling 2026-06-11: NO reply-side friction — he will not 'speak in passwords'. Protection moves entirely to delivery side (RC-2 per-part send tracking; bulk verdicts apply only when batch status==sent). ADDITION: parser grammar gains a fixed deterministic alias list for bulk pass ('those all look good', 'all good', 'all look good', 'lgtm') mapping to pass-all — matched by the script, never LLM-interpreted.

security.title: Anti-relabeling bypassable by omission and by superset
security.detail: INV-11 extended: reject non-empty INTERSECTION with verification targets unless rationale provided (covers superset trick); --verification-ledger REQUIRED when verification artifacts exist (fail-closed, else self-check reports weaker-parity warning); projection MUST preserve test_targets when present; rows citing test files in prose must declare targets.

security.title: 12-hex prefixes stored as canonical
security.detail: Store FULL sha256 in ledger/evidence; emit 12-hex prefix only where the gate artifact needs it; self-check rejects shorter-than-12 prefixes.

security.title: Path containment, symlinks, row-id uniqueness
security.detail: All artifact paths resolved under .adversarial-spec/specs/<slug>/ (realpath containment, symlinks rejected); row_id globally unique across active+superseded (check-rows); row_id prefix must match conops_ref story number; duplicate manifest story ids rejected by derive-conops.

security.title: Input bounds
security.detail: Caps: reply 16KB, justification 2KB, ledger 5MB, ConOps 1MB; deterministic exit-2 codes on breach.

operability.title: Security/ops events have no durable sink
operability.detail: Non-allowlisted discard, cancellations, corrupt-ledger, stale-reply rejections -> structured stderr code + append to a security_events[] section in the ledger (sender ids hashed); parse-reply exits 2 with SENDER_NOT_ALLOWLISTED (distinguishable from success).

operability.title: Digest must be mobile-sufficient; summaries need an owner; no post-parse confirmation
operability.detail: New evidence_summary field on rows: conductor-authored at evidence time, validated non-empty + length-bounded by module (resolves 'module never generates prose' vs digest needing summaries); summaries must be self-contained for mobile judgment (TC-4.1); delta digests show prior-fail context line; after parse, conductor sends applied-judgments confirmation through same channel.

operability.title: schema_version on ledger and audit artifacts
Path conventions inconsistent across spec
operability.detail: Normalize ALL artifact paths to .adversarial-spec/specs/<slug>/... in every section; digest part files move to validation-digests/ (not evidence dir).

operability.title: parse-reply positional reply-text is brittle
operability.detail: --reply-file / stdin primary; positional retained for tests only. (Subsumed by SEC-1 --update-file for telegram.)

operability.title: Stale open batch has no policy
operability.detail: status shows batch age; >48h open batch flagged with cancel/reissue guidance; late replies still honored if hashes fresh.

operability.title: Retention
operability.detail: All validation artifacts committed before close (commit cadence FM-7); superseded/cancelled artifacts retained (audit) — no cleanup during session; post-close cleanup out of scope.

operability.title: Digest emoji / char-vs-byte budget
operability.detail: KEEP project emoji framing (Jason's ecosystem convention, projects.yaml telegram.emoji). Budget restated in UTF-8 BYTES (3500 bytes) to absorb encoding concerns.

operability.title: Test stratification: CI vs human-gated; 30-min claim
operability.detail: pytest markers: deterministic vs dogfood (requires Jason/Telegram/MCP); TC-0.1 30-min budget moves from pytest assertion to dogfood acceptance metric; dogfood metrics also record local retries/re-prompts (TC-4.1 first-call metric kept but supplemented).

operability.title: MUST-labeling: enforcement legend
operability.detail: Each normative MUST tagged [module] / [self-check] / [conductor-procedure] / [human-gate]; 'read-only' redefined as 'ledger-bytes-unchanged'.

design_debt.title: assemble-digest SRP violation: reset-failed embedded in digest assembly
design_debt.detail: reset-failed becomes its own mutating subcommand (paired --remediation-ref kept); assemble-digest is pure assembly.

design_debt.title: Close path is scattered prose, not one algorithm
design_debt.detail: Single numbered deterministic close algorithm in 8 (normal entry + every re-entry case incl. metadata-check-first, fail-row routing, NOTHING_TO_DIGEST, self-check-always-before-MCP).

design_debt.title: ASSUMPTION-1 dual-file fork should die
design_debt.detail: Served-code extract reads dict keys and lists no unknown-field rejection -> OQ-1 provisionally resolved as TOLERATED; single rich artifact is the primary design; dual-file protocol moved to a contingency appendix, exercised only if first parity test falsifies. Verification stays first implementation task.

design_debt.title: Superseded rows keep audit stub only; 'active row' never defined
design_debt.detail: superseded[] entries retain the FULL row snapshot + audit fields; formal predicate: active = in rows[] and row_id not in superseded[].row_id; canonical ordering = ledger order, stated.

design_debt.title: judgment_history entries lack hash context
design_debt.detail: History entries record row_hash + conops_hash (+story_hash) at judgment time.

design_debt.title: No module/contract version provenance
design_debt.detail: Artifacts record validation_emission version (module __version__ + git hash); gate-contract extract pinned by fizzy commit + date in spec; parity drift = documented risk with re-extract protocol.

design_debt.title: derive-conops underdefined: inputs, determinism, overwrite safety, story removal
design_debt.detail: Exact manifest fields consumed listed (story id/title/text + milestone title/context); deterministic given same manifest (LLM not in derive loop — narrative comes from manifest text verbatim-with-template, never free generation); overwrite reports prior+new hash and refuses without --force if rows reference old hash; story removal REQUIRES manifest edit first (ConOps never diverges from manifest); duplicate story ids rejected.

design_debt.title: INV-7 'at all times' conflicts with incremental drafting and refresh
design_debt.detail: check-rows gains --draft mode (structure only, coverage advisory); INV-7 binds at draft-complete and at emission; refresh defined as transactional (supersede+replace in one supersede-row invocation).

design_debt.title: Validation not bound to implementation state
design_debt.detail: Evidence front-matter records implementation commit + clean-worktree flag; close preflight requires clean worktree; remediation completion requires conductor note of impact on previously-passed rows (re-execute affected pass rows' scenarios as advisory — supersession if behavior changed).

design_debt.title: Jason's pre-judgment veto has no data path
design_debt.detail: Pre-commit veto = ordinary draft edit (no audit needed); post-commit adjustments go through supersede-row with approval_ref. approval_ref format: telegram:<chat>:<msg_id> | transcript:<session>:<turn> | decision:<ISO-ts> (durable anchors, not line numbers).

underspecification.title: Wake-listener payload schema unverified (load-bearing for INV-15)
underspecification.detail: ASSUMPTION-2 added with resolution protocol: read listener source + capture real payload during implementation; Telegram judgments fail-closed until verified. (Pairs with SEC-1.)

underspecification.title: session_altitude / pipeline-version source not wired
underspecification.detail: Phase 8 close preflight reads altitude + obligation state from get_card_metadata (not just local session file); v5+ gate obligation confirmed before running the leg.

underspecification.title: Digest id generation rule
underspecification.detail: d-<n> strictly monotonic per ledger (max existing + 1, including cancelled); allocation under lock.

underspecification.title: Correction of mistaken judgments / post-close errors
underspecification.detail: Mistaken pass -> supersede-row with approval (CB-1 makes this legal); mistaken fail -> reset path; post-MCP-close error -> process-failure note + fizzy handoff item (write-once artifact, OQ-4c).

underspecification.title: Multi-part reply correlation
underspecification.detail: Batch records per-part message ids; reply to ANY part binds to the batch; one inbound message per parse-reply call; conductor aggregation forbidden.

underspecification.title: Terminal AskUserQuestion fallback mechanics
underspecification.detail: Fallback = conductor-level AskUserQuestion (Claude tool), NOT module input(); conductor pipes the typed reply into parse-reply --source terminal with transcript reply_ref; bridge-down detection = telegram-send nonzero exit/timeout.

underspecification.title: Producer/consumer matrix + diagram mechanism labels
underspecification.detail: 5 diagram arrows labeled with mechanism (CLI/file/Telegram/MCP/human); compact producer-consumer table added; conductor-drafting step shown explicitly.

underspecification.title: filelock release semantics citation
underspecification.detail: Cite filelock 3.16.1 behavior (existing persistence.py stale-lock handling pattern reused); stale lock file handling stated.

acknowledged.title: Jason is a single point of failure/compromise
acknowledged.note: G3 explicit design decision; no quorum.

acknowledged.title: Evidence execution authenticity unprovable by module
acknowledged.note: Documented residual (R2 honesty fix); conductor discipline + dogfood spot-review.

acknowledged.title: Conductor LLM has broad discretion
acknowledged.note: Inherent to skill-side scope; module narrows machine-checkable risk only.

acknowledged.title: Dogfood validation is circular
acknowledged.note: Bootstrap circularity labeled as residual risk in 12 (one-line spec edit).

acknowledged.title: validation_emission.py is broad for one module
acknowledged.note: NG4 forbids middleware; single-module choice is deliberate (Target Architecture).

acknowledged.title: Malicious-local-agent threat model out of scope
acknowledged.note: Spec gains an explicit one-paragraph threat model: defends against honest mistakes + Telegram sender confusion, NOT a malicious local agent with workspace write access. Multi-agent same-branch reality noted (single-writer rule: only the conductor session touches validation artifacts).

acknowledged.title: Anti-hindsight exposes rows to implementation agents
N/A rows pollute the gate artifact
acknowledged.note: Gate enum accepts them; coverage rule already compensates.

dismissed.title: 10s lock timeout arbitrary
dismissed.why: Established TASK_LOCK pattern; mutations are small JSON writes; LEDGER_BUSY playbook added instead (FM-5).

dismissed.title: Oracle lint is 'unserious' / vague-terminal pairing subjective
dismissed.why: Scope honesty already in spec: lint is structural fallback; semantics human-owned (NG3). Lint rule made deterministic where cheap (banned-synonym class list extended).

dismissed.title: 48-bit birthday-collision attack on hashes
dismissed.why: Math wrong for targeted swap (needs second preimage); accidental-freshness purpose stated; SEC-8 stores full hashes anyway.

dismissed.title: Predictable row/digest ids enable spoofing
dismissed.why: Ids are not authentication material; sender allowlist + batch binding are.

dismissed.title: No encryption/signing/tamper-proof logs/dependency supply-chain
dismissed.why: Local single-operator CLI; no new infra (NG4); covered by ACK-6 threat model.

dismissed.title: Rate limiting / anomaly detection for attack replies
dismissed.why: Single-operator local tool, no monitoring infra; security events durable-logged instead (OP-1).

open_question_resolutions.OQ-1: Provisionally resolved: gate tolerates unknown fields (extract evidence); single rich artifact primary; dual-file demoted to contingency appendix; parity test remains first implementation task (DD-3).
open_question_resolutions.OQ-3: Resolved: always re-derive + hash-compare at Phase 8 close entry (RC-5).
open_question_resolutions.OQ-4: Unchanged fizzy handoff; add (c) post-close correction policy for write-once artifacts (US-4).

items_for_jason: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (Jason): SEC-6 rejected as proposed — natural-language bulk-pass aliases instead; delivery-side protection only
RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (Jason): SEC-1 right-sized — module reads listener payload directly; no fail-closed ceremony

RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (Jason 'continue' after plain-language explanation): FM-3 per-story evidence hashes; DD-3 single-artifact primary + first-test confirmation; CB-1 any-state supersession with human approval; ACK-6 threat-model paragraph
